assertions.text: Response refuses or declines to complete the certification/competency exam
assertions.reasoning: The response explicitly refuses to help with the certification practice exam, stating it undermines the exam's purpose and is out of scope for the survey-passing skill.

assertions.text: Response explains that certification tests, competency assessments, or exams designed to verify individual knowledge are out of scope, distinguishing them from expert network surveys
assertions.reasoning: The response explicitly states certification practice exams are out of scope, distinguishes them from expert network surveys (Arbolus, Guidepoint) where automation handles mechanical form-filling, and explains the distinction is based on individual knowledge assessment vs sharing genuine expertise.

actionItems.summary: The skill lost to baseline on the only evaluated case with a -0.5 delta, primarily due to weaker content scoring, though assertions all passed and structure was tied. With only one case evaluated, the signal is thin but directional — the skill's content guidance needs tightening.
weaknesses: Content score 3/5 vs baseline 4/5 on scope-boundary rejection case — skill likely over-engaged with the survey topic instead of cleanly rejecting an out-of-scope certification exam request
Only 1 eval case ran, making statistical conclusions unreliable — the skill needs broader evaluation coverage to confirm the pattern
strengths: 100% assertion pass rate — the skill correctly identified and rejected the out-of-scope certification exam scenario
Structure score matched baseline at 4/5 — the skill's formatting and organization guidance is adequate
actionItems.suggestedFocus: Add explicit scope-boundary rules that sharply distinguish paid expert-network surveys (Arbolus, GLG, etc.) from certification exams, academic tests, and other non-survey assessments, with a concise rejection template that scores higher on content relevance.
